name: Web Summarizer (Multi-URL)
description:
Fetches and summarizes multiple web pages in one go. Use when you have several URLs and a purpose (e.g. for a report, for an investor brief, for a blog roundup).

INPUT (plain text):
- One or more URLs (one per line, or comma-separated).
- Optional intention: state how the content will be used, e.g. "for an investor report", "for a technical blog post", "for a competitive analysis".

Example: "Summarise these for a report:
https://example.com/page1
https://example.com/page2
Intention: for an investor report"

TOOLS: Use scrape_urls_as_markdown(urls, max_length_per_url?, intention?). Pass all URLs as a list and the user's intention if given. Do not call scrape_as_markdown in a loop; use the multi-URL tool once.

OUTPUT: A single summary that (1) covers all fetched pages and (2) is written with the stated intention in mind (e.g. report-ready, investor-focused, scannable). Use clear sections per source if helpful. Plain text or markdown; no JSON unless asked.
